title: 10 best herbal essential oils for arteriosclerosis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for arteriosclerosis are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of this condition by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the arteries. The best herbal essential oils for arteriosclerosis include Ginkgo biloba, Salvia miltiorrhiza, Panax ginseng, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Panax quinquefolius, Boswellia serrata, Curcuma longa, Lavandula angustifolia, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, arteriosclerosis can lead to heart attacks, strokes, and kidney disease, severely impacting one's quality of life, causing fatigue, pain, and limited mobility, ultimately affecting daily activities and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which are hallmark features of arteriosclerosis. The oils' ability to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure also contributes to their therapeutic effects. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's sesquiterpenes have been shown to inhibit the proliferation of smooth muscle cells, a key factor in the development of atherosclerotic plaques. This holistic approach may help to slow down or even reverse arteriosclerosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: The flavonoids and other polyphenols present in Maidenhair fern extracts may help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.

Vasodilatory effects: Some studies suggest that compounds found in Maidenhair fern extracts, such as sesquiterpenes, may help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which could be beneficial for reducing blood pressure and improving circulation in individuals with arteriosclerosis.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The saponins and other glycosides present in Maidenhair fern extracts may have anti-inflammatory properties that could help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and prevent damage caused by arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots have been traditionally used to create essential oils that help to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation, making them beneficial for treating arteriosclerosis.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and saponins that help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, making it a valuable component in essential oil blends for arteriosclerosis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves at dawn when dewy to ensure highest quality and potency.
Dry the fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat setting.
Steam distill 50 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 500 milliliters of distilled water for 2 hours to extract oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter paper into a clean glass bottle to remove impurities.
Store the final herbal ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby slowing down the progression of the disease. The oils also have a vasodilating effect, which means they can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the risk of cardiovascular events. Additionally, sage essential oils may help lower cholesterol levels and improve lipid profiles, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing arteriosclerosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Herbal sage essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the development and progression of arteriosclerosis by reducing oxidative stress in the blood vessels.

Inflammation Reduction: Sage essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the arteries, which is a key factor in the development of arteriosclerosis. By reducing inflammation, sage essential oil may help prevent further damage to blood vessels.

Cholesterol Regulation: Herbal sage essential oil may help regulate cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and increasing the production of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol, which can help reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used due to their rich concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of sage contains a high amount of volatile compounds that have been shown to help lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces and mix with 100ml of high-proof vegetable oil such as olive or grapeseed oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Leave the mixture to macerate in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking occasionally, before straining through cheesecloth.
Filter the resulting essential oil through activated charcoal and store it in a dark glass bottle with an airtight cap for up to 6 months or until fully consumed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation in the arteries can help prevent the buildup of plaque, a major contributor to arteriosclerosis. Additionally, curry plant essential oils' antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that damage artery walls, reducing the risk of hardening and narrowing of the arteries. By addressing these underlying causes, curry plant essential oils may help promote healthy blood flow and reduce the risk of heart disease associated with arteriosclerosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: The essential oil of Curry Plant has been shown to have potent antioxidant activity, which can help protect against the oxidative damage caused by free radicals in the body, thereby reducing the progression of Arteriosclerosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Curry Plant essential oil has been found to exhibit significant anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thereby slowing down the development of Arteriosclerosis.

Antioxidant-vascular protective effects: The oil's antioxidant-vascular protective effects can also help protect against damage to blood vessel walls, reducing the risk of plaque buildup and the subsequent development of Arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create essential oils that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can contribute to alleviating symptoms of arteriosclerosis.

Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh helichrysum italicum leaves in the early morning dew.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours without humidity control.
Combine the dried helichrysum italicum leaves with 1000ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3.
Infuse the mixture in a dark glass container at room temperature for 21 days shaking daily.
Strain and filter the mixture using cheesecloth or coffee filters and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This inflammation is a major contributor to the development of arteriosclerosis, as it causes the arteries to become narrowed and stiffened, leading to reduced blood flow. The antioxidant properties of basil essential oil also help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can damage the arteries and contribute to the progression of the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Basil essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and damage to blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in basil essential oil, such as eugenol and linalool, help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can prevent further narrowing or blockage of arteries.

Cardiovascular-Protective Compounds: Basil essential oil contains compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which have been shown to have cardiovascular-protective effects by improving blood flow, reducing platelet aggregation, and inhibiting the formation of blood clots.
remedy_parts: Roots: Basil roots have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them a potential candidate for treating arteriosclerosis.

Buds: The buds of the basil plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers by cutting them at dawn when their oil content is highest.
Steam distill the plant material using 100 grams of ocimum basilicum for every 500 milliliters of distilled water.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol through decantation or filtration.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 20 degrees celsius for at least three months.
Use a high-quality carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to dilute the essential oil to a concentration of 2-3 percent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation. The antioxidant properties in lemon balm help to protect against oxidative stress, which can damage the inner lining of arteries and contribute to the formation of plaque. Vasodilatory: Lemon balm essential oil's vasodilatory properties can help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure, both of which are beneficial for reversing the effects of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arteriosclerosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or late afternoon in dry weather conditions weighing approximately 50 grams.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and gently pat them dry using a clean towel.
Steam distill the washed leaves in a laboratory-grade still for 30 minutes to release the essential oils using 10 milliliters of water per 1 gram of plant material.
Collect the obtained essential oil and separate it from the distillate by decantation or filtering through a sterile cotton cloth within 24 hours of production.
Bottle the pure melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at the peak of bloom for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels or cloth screens within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers using a glass still and deionized water at a ratio of 1:4 for 2-3 hours.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distillate using a separator funnel within 30 minutes.
Transfer the extracted essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool dry place.

Dry the washed thymus vulgaris leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves using a steam distillation apparatus, collecting the essential oil after 2 hours of distillation.
Filter and bottle the collected essential oil using a coffee filter paper, storing it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for future use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat arteriosclerosis with herbal essential oils is by blending Helichrysum italicum, Lavender angustifolia, and Frankincense sacra oils. remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because it has been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba essential oil, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to protect the blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage. This, in turn, helps to improve the flexibility of the blood vessels, reducing the risk of blood clots and improving overall cardiovascular health. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba essential oil may also help to slow the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, thereby preventing the progression of arteriosclerosis.
Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to protect blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.

Ginkgolides: These diterpenes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to improve blood flow and reduce the risk of blood clots, thereby slowing the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils for Ginkgo biloba, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.

Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are also used to create essential oils, which are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids that help to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress associated with arteriosclerosis.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain terpenoids and flavonoids that are used to create essential oils, which are believed to help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them beneficial for arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves in early autumn when they are fully ripe and dry.
Steam distill the ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of distilled water for 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water phase and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid at room temperature for 2 months.
Inspect the essential oil for clarity and aroma before using it in aromatherapy or perfumery applications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in blood vessels. The oil's active compounds, such as tanshinones, have been shown to relax blood vessels, increase nitric oxide production, and enhance the bioavailability of nitric oxide. This leads to vasodilation and improved blood flow, reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis. The oil's antioxidant properties also help protect against oxidative stress and lipid peroxidation, further contributing to the prevention and treatment of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinone: Helps to improve blood circulation and reduce blood pressure, which can slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis.
Rosmarinic acid: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Ligustrazine (Norbellidrine): Helps to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Salvia miltiorrhiza are primarily used to create essential oils due to their high content of tanshinones, which are known for their cardiovascular benefits.
Leaves: The leaves of Salvia miltiorrhiza are also commonly used for essential oils, containing various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids that support cardiovascular health.
Barks: The barks of Salvia miltiorrhiza are another key component in essential oils, rich in salvianolic acids and other compounds that contribute to their arteriosclerosis-preventing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature salvia miltiorrhiza roots in large quantities after 2 years of cultivation to obtain the highest essential oil content.
Steam distill the harvested roots for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent degradation and contamination.
Label and date the bottle with the essential oil name and concentration to ensure accurate identification and usage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because it contains compounds that may help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. The oil's antioxidant properties may also help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis. Additionally, the oil's potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health may also play a role in mitigating the progression of arteriosclerosis and promoting overall vascular well-being. remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These ginsenosides have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thus helping to prevent the formation of plaque that leads to arteriosclerosis.

Saponins: Saponins in Panax ginseng have been found to inhibit the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is involved in the regulation of blood pressure and can contribute to the development of arteriosclerosis.

Terpenoids (specifically, ginsenolic acid and ginsenoside Rg1): These terpenoids have been demonstrated to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arteriosclerosis.

Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are also used for essential oil production, containing compounds like phenolic acids, which may help prevent the formation of blood clots and reduce inflammation in blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh ginseng roots, clean them with water, and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginseng roots at 100-150°c for 4-6 hours to release the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water using a separatory funnel and collect the oil in a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and residue from the distillation process.
Store the herbal panax ginseng essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from heat and light sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The primary part of Zingiber officinale used to make essential oils for arteriosclerosis is due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils for arteriosclerosis, as they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.

Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separatory funnel after 30 minutes.

Dry the harvested roots in a warm, well-ventilated area at a temperature of 35-40°c for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 500g of dried panax quinquefolius roots in a distillation apparatus at 100°c for 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil using a separator, discarding the water and plant material.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and then transfer to a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure. The oil's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines also contributes to its beneficial effects. Additionally, Boswellia serrata has been shown to improve the flexibility of blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood flow and reduced risk of blood clots, ultimately helping to alleviate the symptoms of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This triterpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the arterial walls, thereby preventing the progression of arteriosclerosis.

β-Boswellic acids: These triterpenoid compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce the formation of atherosclerotic plaques and prevent the narrowing of blood vessels.

α-Boswellic acids: These triterpenoid compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arteriosclerosis, thereby preventing the progression of the disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high amounts of boswellic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in treating arteriosclerosis.
Rhyzomes: They are also a rich source of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of arteriosclerosis, such as reduced blood flow and inflammation.

Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, which help to inhibit platelet aggregation, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow, making them useful in treating arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin from mature trees, typically in the summer months when the resin is at its peak.
Steam distill the collected resin at 100-150 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel, then filter it through a paper filter.
Transfer the filtered oil to a dark glass bottle and seal it tightly, store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
Check the oil's quality and purity using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compound, curcumin, inhibits the formation of plaque in blood vessels, thereby reducing the risk of blockages and inflammation. Additionally, curcuma longa essential oil helps to improve blood lipid profiles by lowering triglycerides and increasing high-density lipoprotein (HDL) levels. Its cardiovascular benefits are attributed to its ability to relax blood vessels, improve blood circulation, and enhance the body's natural antioxidant defenses. This contributes to overall cardiovascular health and reduced risk of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with arteriosclerosis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of atherosclerosis and plaque buildup in blood vessels.
Ar-turmerone: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent the formation of foam cells and reduce the severity of atherosclerosis.
Bisabolol: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against endothelial dysfunction and reduce the progression of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help prevent and treat arteriosclerosis.
Roots: Roots are used because they also contain curcumin, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain volatile oils, including beta-pinene and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help prevent arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa roots in late afternoon when the plant's volatile oils are at their highest concentration.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Steam distill the cleaned roots at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a glass container and filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Store the obtained curcuma longa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potential vasodilatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's high linalool and linalyl acetate content may help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress, which contributes to the development of arteriosclerosis. By improving cardiovascular health and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help alleviate symptoms associated with arteriosclerosis and promote overall cardiovascular well-being. This makes it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an antioxidant that helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, contributing to the prevention of arteriosclerosis.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect against the formation of plaque and improve blood vessel function.

Limonene: Has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis by protecting against the formation of atherosclerotic plaques and improving blood vessel health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with arteriosclerosis.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and cardiovascular-protective properties, which can help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Stems: They are used for their flavonoid content, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect against arteriosclerosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator at 35-40 degrees celsius for 24 to 48 hours.
Steam distill 1 kilogram of dried flowers in a 5-liter distillation apparatus for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and separate it from the water.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with arteriosclerosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's high content of salvenic acid, saponins, and flavonoids helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system. This, in turn, may help to slow down the progression of arteriosclerosis by preventing the buildup of plaque in arteries. The oil's vasodilatory properties also aid in improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure, further contributing to the prevention and management of arteriosclerosis.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Helps with arteriosclerosis by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can protect against the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the development of atherosclerosis.
Caryophyllene oxide: Helps with arteriosclerosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and eicosanoids, which can contribute to the progression of atherosclerotic lesions.

Rosmarinic acid: Helps with arteriosclerosis by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as by modulating lipid metabolism and preventing the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils that help prevent arteriosclerosis.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce the risk of arteriosclerosis.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to retain the bioactive compounds found in the leaves and flowers, making them a suitable alternative for essential oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil quality and potency.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to separate the essential oil from the water.
Collect 10ml of the distilled essential oil that separates from the distillate and store it in a dark glass vial.
Filter the collected essential oil through a sterile filter paper to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.
